title: WIP: Wrote pod ps, rm, create, start. Added tests, man page and completions.
body:
These commands were the minimal functionality needed to test any of them, so I waited to create PR until each were totally finished.
A pod can now be created, started, listed and removed.

Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Some of the flag names I am a bit iffy on, so I'm labeling this as a WIP for now. 
These are the my main name convention ponderings:
podman pod ps has the flags --ctr-names, and --ctr-ids
podman pod rm --rmctr is the way to remove containers as well
podman pod start has no flags as of now (I have been wondering if it is possible to attach a container after starting but I haven't figured that out yet)
podman pod create has cgroup-to-ctr, cgroup-parent and podidfile

Other things I'm not sure about: 
The man pages are having trouble with subcommands. I can view with
```man podman-pod-rm```
but currently
```man podman pod rm``` gives me podman pod and rm seperately.

I'm not totally sure what should be included as default in podman ps
and podman ps format is difficult with the complete name ({{.ContainerNames}} for instance)

@mheon @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 @rhatdan I'd love feedback please :)
I don't think attaching to a container after starting a pod makes sense - we potentially have many containers in a pod, so which would we attach to?
`ps` should probably include the status of containers in the pod - how many are stopped, running, created, paused, etc.
@haircommander I think it would be better to break this PR into several PRs to make them easier to review and track.

I think create/start/rm can safely be in one PR. There aren't many flags to debate, and the code should be relatively simple (build a set of pods to operate on, and invoke the appropriate API on the pod end). ps probably deserves separate handling.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1065) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
closed because a new PR will be made for start and stop
